text: Interesting! Thanks. Something I'm focusing more on in 2021 is to get the conversation started about #abolishcopyright and #abolishpatents. I don't want to have that discussion here, since it can get pretty political, however I have found that there are a lot of people who have come to the conclusions that I have, that reform isn't the way to go and we need to fully abolish. Given that there are no organizations yet (at least in the US, AFAIK—I think worldwide the Pirate Party may be more established), working on that and giving a voice to people who have that stance, if you happen to already agree feel free to get in touch (<mailto:breck7@gmail.com|breck7@gmail.com>). Again, if you don't have that stance would love to debate it, but not here. Think politics should stay out of FOC :slightly_smiling_face:

text: Yeah, I agree that software copyright and software patents in particular stretch old legal concepts past their breaking point.

elements.text: Yeah, I agree that software copyright and software patents in particular stretch old legal concepts past their breaking point.

text: Going back to OP, the second half is an extremely comprehensive list! It's kinda interesting to think about what fraction of those points modern corporations satisfy. On one hand, if we got them all open source would eat the world. On the other, perfect may be the enemy of good enough here.

text:
I unironically agree with <@UBN9AFS0N>. As somebody who went straight from managed hosting (cpanel) to aws, now that I've started managing a site by sshing into a box running apache, I've hit far fewer roadblocks, and the ones I've hit have been simpler to sort out.

That being said, I recently made a google sheet act as a basic cms for a site. So that could be an option too.

elements.text: I unironically agree with

. As somebody who went straight from managed hosting (cpanel) to aws, now that I've started managing a site by sshing into a box running apache, I've hit far fewer roadblocks, and the ones I've hit have been simpler to sort out.

That being said, I recently made a google sheet act as a basic cms for a site. So that could be an option too.

text:
<@UP00ZLX6G> I’m intrigued by your suggestion! I think you can trigger AWS Lambda or Github Action via Zapier/etc based on entries to Google Forms/Airtable. And presumably you can force people to log-in with their google identity for auth purposes & record that.
I wish I thought of that two days ago :stuck_out_tongue:
Only thing I’m not sure about is how do you enforce “unique per user field” (e.g. username)

text:
I’m not convinced by “forego static+serverless” requirement & just spin up the server :slightly_smiling_face:
Looked up what it’d take to do that in Python+Flask (as I’d go there given my background vs php). And while I think is/can be/ conceptually simpler, There is a lot more code to write & infra to properly setup. & more complexity lurks around the corner..

text: For a product entirely about writing music, it is surprising there was no audio feedback included in their demo.

attachments.title: Want to Reverse Inequality? Change Intellectual Property Rules.
attachments.text: Changes in IP have done far more than tax cuts to increase inequality—and US protection of IP could lead to a cold war with China.
attachments.fallback: The Nation: Want to Reverse Inequality? Change Intellectual Property Rules.

elements.text: https://www.thenation.com/article/economy/inequality-patents-taxes-copyright/
